preamble: The unique historical shift from a palatalized alveolar trill [rʲ] to a voiced retroflex fricative [ʐ]. This is a defining characteristic of Polish phonological history.

examples.note: The digraph 'rz' evolved from a soft 'r' into a sound identical to 'ż'.

sources: Swan, O. E. (2002). A Grammar of Contemporary Polish.
Shevelov, G. Y. (1964). A Prehistory of Slavic.
